witnesses.description: A finite free abelian chain complex C1 = Z<e>, C0 = Z<v> with d1(e) = 2v and d0 = 0.
Smith-normal-form data showing H0 is Z/2 and H1 is zero for d1 = [2].
The class of v has order two: 2v is a boundary, but v is not.

checks.claim: The integer boundary maps d1(e)=2v and d0=0 form a chain complex because d0*d1 is zero.
checks.notes: The validator checks basis sizes, boundary-matrix shape, and the listed zero boundary composition exactly over the integers.

checks.claim: The boundary matrix [2] has Smith diagonal [2], so H0 has no free part and one Z/2 torsion factor while H1 has rank zero.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the rank over Q, checks the listed one-entry Smith diagonal, and verifies the free-rank and torsion-factor bookkeeping.

checks.claim: The class [v] in H0 has order two: 2v is in im(d1), but v is not in im(d1).
checks.notes: The validator checks that d1(1*e)=2v and that the single-generator boundary equation 2*k=1 has no integer solution.

checks.claim: The generator v is a boundary in the complex d1(e)=2v.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the fixed divisibility obstruction: the image of d1 is 2Z, so coefficient 1 is not a boundary.

checks.claim: There is an integer k with 2*k = 1, making v a boundary.

data.certificate: produce_diophantine_evidence emits Evidence::UnsatDiophantine checked by Evidence::check for the integer equation 2k = 1

checks.notes: The SMT-LIB artifact isolates the torsion-generator error as a one-variable integer Diophantine contradiction.

checks.claim: General universal-coefficient and homology-invariance theorems require future proof resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: universal coefficient theorem, Ext/Tor functor laws, chain-homotopy invariance, exact sequences, and topological invariance of torsion homology
data.future_checker: Lean modules for chain complexes over Z, finitely generated abelian groups, Smith normal form, Ext/Tor, and proof reconstruction from finite quotient certificates

checks.notes: The finite rows check one explicit chain complex and one torsion obstruction; general algebraic-topology theorem statements stay in the Lean-horizon lane.
